title: Chest infection follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.
investigations.rationale: Supports qualified follow-up chest radiography for chest infection follow-up; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.

referral.text: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.
referral.rationale: Supports qualified pneumonia reassessment for chest infection follow-up; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.

follow_up.text: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.
follow_up.rationale: Supports pneumonia recovery and when to seek further advice for chest infection follow-up; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.4 — recovery and when to seek further advice

additions.text: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

additions.evidence_extract: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Original diagnosis, organism, treatment, interval symptoms, fever, breathlessness, oxygenation, examination, tests, recovery, complications and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for chest infection follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
